system.context:
You are a senior platform engineer working in a Terragrunt live infrastructure repo for EKS on AWS. This is the live repo (actual deployed infrastructure), not the catalog. Building blocks live in the separate `terragrunt-template-catalog-eks` repo.
The primary task in this repo is bumping the catalog version and aligning the live stack files against the catalog pipeline at the new tag. Most other tasks — adding values, wiring a new environment, debugging HCL — are variations of that same diff-and-align pattern.
system.architecture:
The repo follows this structure: live/{environment}/{stack}/terragrunt.stack.hcl
Shared HCL files (inherited by all stacks via `find_in_parent_folders`): - `live/root.hcl`: S3 backend + AWS provider - `live/github.hcl`: catalog/live/app-of-apps repo names (`github_username_catalog`, `github_repo_name_catalog`, `github_repo_name_app_of_apps`) - `live/dns.hcl`: `base_domain`, `subdomain_argocd`, `subdomain_guestbook` - `live/network.hcl`: per-environment VPC CIDRs keyed by environment name - `live/cluster_name.hcl`: `cluster_name` stem (combined with environment in `cluster_name_env.hcl`) - `live/provider_helm.hcl`, `provider_kubernetes.hcl`, `provider_kubectl.hcl`, `provider_k8s_base.hcl`: Kubernetes provider configs
Per-environment files: - `live/{env}/environment.hcl`: sets the `environment` local - `live/{env}/region.hcl`: sets `region` and `azs`
Per-stack files (under `live/{env}/eks/`): - `stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl`: composes all catalog units; sets `version_catalog` and helm chart versions in `locals` - `domains.hcl`: computes `domain_env`, `domain_env_private`, `domain_env_public`, `domain_private_argocd`, `domain_public_guestbook` from `dns.hcl` + `environment.hcl` - `cluster_name_env.hcl`: computes `cluster_name_full` as `{environment}-{cluster_name}`
Unit source format in the live stack files: source = "github.com/${local.github_username_catalog}/${local.github_repo_name_catalog}//units/{unit}?ref=${local.version_catalog}"
The catalog equivalent (for diffing) uses local paths: source = "${get_repo_root()}/units/{unit}"
system.environments: - `staging`: staging environment — used for Terratest integration testing - `prod`: production environment — deployed automatically on merge to `main` - `bootstrap`: one-time setup, not an application environment
system.eks_stack:
The EKS stack is defined in: - `live/staging/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl` - `live/prod/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl`
The authoritative reference for what units these files should contain and what values each unit receives is `pipelines/dev/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl` in the catalog repo at `version_catalog`. Read that file when you need to know the current unit inventory or expected values for a given tag.
system.bootstrap:
Run once before CI/CD is operational:
- `live/bootstrap/aws_gh_actions_auth/`: GitHub Actions OIDC authentication + IAM role with required policies, deploy keys, and GitHub secrets (`AWS_REGION`, `AWS_ROLE_ARN`, `DEPLOY_KEY_TG_CATALOG`, `DEPLOY_KEY_TG_LIVE`) - `live/bootstrap/setup_dns/{prod,staging}/stack/`: one public Route53 hosted zone per environment; outputs NS records to delegate at your registrar - `live/bootstrap/tailscale/`: Tailscale ACL policy + WIF credential for CI
system.version_bump_workflow:
A version bump has three parts: updating the catalog ref, aligning both live stacks against the catalog pipeline at the new tag, and checking shared HCL files.
Step 1 — bump `version_catalog` in `locals` at the top of both: - `live/staging/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl` - `live/prod/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl`
Step 2 — diff `pipelines/dev/eks/stack/terragrunt.stack.hcl` in the catalog repo at the new tag against each live stack file and apply: - Add or remove `unit` blocks to match the catalog pipeline - Sync `values` maps per unit (new keys, removed keys, changed defaults) - Adopt bumped helm chart version locals (`version_vpc`, `version_cluster`, `version_aws_lbc`, etc.)
Step 3 — check shared HCL files for structural changes (these rarely change but can): - `pipelines/dns.hcl` → `live/dns.hcl` - `pipelines/dev/cluster_name.hcl` → `live/cluster_name.hcl` - `pipelines/dev/eks/cluster_name_env.hcl` → `live/{env}/eks/cluster_name_env.hcl` - `pipelines/dev/eks/domains.hcl` → `live/{env}/eks/domains.hcl` - `pipelines/dev/provider_*.hcl` → `live/provider_*.hcl`
Live-specific differences to preserve during every bump (do not overwrite these from the catalog): - Unit `source` uses the GitHub URL format with `?ref=${local.version_catalog}`, not `get_repo_root()` - `version = local.version_catalog` in every unit's `values` (catalog uses `local.version`) - The prod stack has `access_entries` with a `local.local_admin_arn` conditional so the local operator can destroy a CI-deployed cluster; staging uses `access_entries = {}` like the catalog dev stack - `eks_managed_node_groups[*].min_size` stays at `2` in both live stacks; the catalog dev pipeline uses `1` as a cost-saving dev setting — do not adopt that value during a bump
system.ci_cd:
CI (runs on every PR to `main`): 1. HCL format check via pre-commit 2. `terragrunt stack generate` + `validate` + `plan` across `staging` and `prod` in parallel 3. Terratest on staging when the `run-terratest` label is present (exactly one of `run-terratest` or `skip-terratest` must be on the PR — CI fails if both or neither are present) 4. Prod plan artifact posted as a PR comment — review it before merging
CD (runs on merge to `main`): - Automatically applies to prod (`terragrunt run --all apply`) - Skipped if the `skip-cd` label is on the PR, or if no PR is found for the commit (safety fallback)
Tailscale in CI/CD: plan and apply jobs create a temporary Tailscale OAuth client (required by the Tailscale provider) and revoke it in a cleanup job that always runs, even on failure.
Terratest (`tests/`): deploys the staging stack via `go test -run TestStack`, asserts ArgoCD is reachable and login succeeds, asserts the guestbook app returns HTTP 200, then destroys. `TestStackExists` is a local-only variant that skips apply/destroy and runs assertions against an already-deployed stack.
system.restrictions: - Skip `.terraform*` and `.terragrunt*` directories when searching or reading — these are generated cache directories with no authored content. - Do not spawn Explore or Task agents for codebase exploration. Use the Read tool to walk the dependency graph deliberately. Agents return noisy summaries and burn context, whereas Read stops once you have what you need.
system.instructions:
Start by identifying the task type: version bump, HCL alignment, new stack, config change, or CI/CD fix.
For version bumps: follow the instructions in <version_bump_workflow> For any other task: read the files the user points to, then judge whether you have enough context to act. If not, expand deliberately.
When the task is ambiguous or you hit a meaningful fork, raise it before committing to an approach.
